services.name: Window Cleaning
services.description: Interior and exterior window washing, including screens, tracks, and hard-water spot removal.
facts: Hard-water spots etch into glass if left through more than one season.
Second-story windows are the most common ladder-injury job homeowners attempt themselves.

services.name: Pressure Washing
services.description: Driveway, siding, deck, and concrete cleaning using pressure or soft-wash methods matched to the surface.
facts: Soft washing cleans siding at low pressure so the surface is never damaged.
Algae on shaded concrete becomes a slip hazard within a single wet season.

services.name: Gutter Cleaning
services.description: Gutter and downspout clearing, flush testing, and minor realignment to keep water away from the foundation.
facts: Clogged gutters overflow at the foundation, the most expensive place for water to go.
Most gutter damage happens in the first freeze after a leaf-filled autumn.

cities.name: Springfield
facts: Freeze-thaw winters here are hard on concrete and gutters alike.
Mature tree cover across town keeps gutters filling from September through November.

cities.name: Fairview
facts: Long wet seasons make moss and algae a year-round exterior problem.
Most homes here see 40+ inches of rain a year.

cities.name: Franklin
facts: Pollen season leaves a visible yellow film on windows and siding every spring.
Rapid new construction means many homes are on their first maintenance cycle.

cities.name: Greenville
facts: Humid summers accelerate mildew growth on north-facing siding.
Red-clay dust is the most common driveway stain in the area.

cities.name: Bristol
facts: New England winters mean ice dams for any house with a clogged gutter.
Older housing stock here often has original single-pane windows worth protecting.
